properties.role: user with a saved active plan
user who opens a shared URL

properties.kind: thrown by decoder on bad input
properties.codes: EMPTY | INVALID_BASE64 | INVALID_GZIP | INVALID_JSON | INVALID_SCHEMA | UNSUPPORTED_VERSION

scenarios.description: Sender generates a share URL from a valid saved plan
scenarios.given: the user is on /plan and the active plan has at least one named exercise
scenarios.when: the user clicks the Share button
then: a modal opens showing a read-only input with the full share URL
the URL has the form <origin><base>share#data=<payload>
the modal shows the exercise count and an approximate encoded byte size
no IndexedDB write happens

scenarios.description: Share button is disabled when plan is unsaveable
scenarios.given: the active plan is null OR has zero exercises with a non-empty name
scenarios.when: the user views the plan editor
then: the Share button is rendered but disabled
the Save flow is unaffected

scenarios.given: the share modal is open with a URL in its input
then: the URL is written to the system clipboard via navigator.clipboard.writeText
the button label changes to 'Copied!' for ~1.5s, then reverts

scenarios.description: Recipient opens a valid share URL and previews the plan
scenarios.given: the URL hash is '#data=<valid base64url-encoded gzip JSON v1 payload>'
then: the payload is decoded into { v: 1, exercises: [...] }
each exercise is rendered with name, sets, reps, rest seconds
instructions are collapsed by default and expandable
Import and Cancel buttons are visible

scenarios.given: the share page shows a valid decoded preview
then: planRepo.savePlan is called with the normalised exercises from the payload
on success, the hash is cleared from the URL and the user is navigated to /plan
the new plan is visible in the editor
session history is untouched

scenarios.given: the share page shows a decoded preview
scenarios.when: the user clicks Cancel, presses Escape, or clicks the backdrop
then: the user is navigated to / with the hash cleared
no IndexedDB write happens

scenarios.description: Recipient opens a corrupt or truncated share URL
scenarios.given: the URL hash is '#data=###not-valid-base64url###' or a payload that fails gzip/JSON/schema checks
then: the page shows the message 'This share link is invalid or corrupted.'
a link back to / is rendered
no Import or Cancel button is shown

scenarios.description: Recipient opens a share URL from a newer app version
scenarios.given: the payload decodes to JSON with { v: 2, exercises: [...] }
then: the page shows 'This share link was created with a newer version of the app.'
no Import or Cancel button is shown
a link back to / is rendered

scenarios.given: a plan object with N normalised exercises
scenarios.when: encodePlanToPayload is called and the result is passed to decodePayloadToPlan
then: decodePayloadToPlan returns { v: 1, exercises: [...] }
the returned exercises are structurally equal to the input exercises after normaliseExercise

scenarios.description: encodePlanToPayload produces a base64url string with no padding
scenarios.given: a plan with at least one exercise
then: the returned string matches /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/
the returned string contains no '+', '/', or '=' characters

scenarios.description: decodePayloadToPlan throws SharePayloadError with INVALID_BASE64 on bad base64url
scenarios.given: a payload string containing characters outside [A-Za-z0-9_-]
then: it throws SharePayloadError with code 'INVALID_BASE64'

scenarios.description: decodePayloadToPlan throws SharePayloadError with INVALID_GZIP on bad gzip bytes
scenarios.given: a valid base64url string that does not decode to valid gzip
then: it throws SharePayloadError with code 'INVALID_GZIP'

scenarios.description: decodePayloadToPlan throws SharePayloadError with INVALID_JSON on non-JSON gzip content
scenarios.given: valid base64url + valid gzip wrapping a non-JSON string
then: it throws SharePayloadError with code 'INVALID_JSON'

scenarios.description: decodePayloadToPlan throws SharePayloadError with UNSUPPORTED_VERSION on v>1
scenarios.given: a payload whose decoded JSON has v=2
then: it throws SharePayloadError with code 'UNSUPPORTED_VERSION'

scenarios.description: decodePayloadToPlan throws SharePayloadError with INVALID_SCHEMA on missing fields
scenarios.given: a payload whose decoded JSON has v=1 but exercises is missing or not an array
then: it throws SharePayloadError with code 'INVALID_SCHEMA'

scenarios.description: extractPayloadFromHash returns the substring after #data= URL-decoded
scenarios.when: extractPayloadFromHash is called with that hash
then: it returns 'eyJ2IjoxLCJleGVyY2lzZXMiOltdfQ'

scenarios.description: extractPayloadFromHash returns null when the hash does not carry data
scenarios.given: window.location.hash is '', '#', or anything not starting with '#data='
then: it returns null
it does not throw

scenarios.description: buildShareUrl composes origin, base, route, and payload
scenarios.when: buildShareUrl is called with those arguments
then: it returns 'https://x.test/p/share#data=abc'
it never reads window

scenarios.description: End-to-end: sender generates URL, opens it in a new context, imports, sees new plan
scenarios.given: the editor has a saved plan with two named exercises
scenarios.when: the user clicks Share, copies the URL, opens the URL in a new tab, clicks Import
then: the share page shows the two named exercises in order
after Import, navigating to /plan shows those two exercises in the editor
the URL hash is empty after import

v.description: Schema version. 1 is the only version defined by this feature.

exercises.description: Exercise records in display order. Each entry is normalised by normaliseExercise before storage.

code.description: One of EMPTY | INVALID_BASE64 | INVALID_GZIP | INVALID_JSON | INVALID_SCHEMA | UNSUPPORTED_VERSION.

message.description: Human-readable message safe to display to the user.

origin.description: window.location.origin at the time of share.

base.description: Configured Vite base path with trailing slash, e.g. '/personal-gym/'.

nonFunctionalRequirements: Encoding + decoding a 20-exercise plan completes in under 50ms on a mid-range mobile device.
Encoded URL stays under 2000 characters for plans of up to 20 exercises.
No new runtime dependencies are introduced; the pipeline uses CompressionStream, DecompressionStream, TextEncoder, TextDecoder, and atob.
The share pipeline is unit-testable in jsdom (the encoding module is structured so the compression step is swappable in tests).
PWA offline mode works: once the app shell is cached, the entire share/import flow is fully functional without network access.
The hash fragment is used (not the query string) so the payload is never sent to any server in Referer headers.
assumptions: The existing single-active-plan model is preserved; this feature does not introduce a plan library.
Recipients have a compatible browser that supports CompressionStream and DecompressionStream; the app already targets only browsers that do.
crypto.randomUUID is available in jsdom during tests (or polyfilled via the existing test setup).
Existing planModel.normaliseExercise is the single source of truth for exercise shape on import.
The Vite BASE constant is already centralised enough that the share UI can import it without a circular dependency.
